target.name: Aurora Innovation
target.founder: Chris Urmson, Sterling Anderson, Drew Bagnell

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Aurora Innovation operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector at the public stage, having raised over $2B to date. The comparable universe consists of six private firms in the same sector, including Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, and ideaForge. No public peers exist in this narrow category, limiting traditional peer-median valuation approaches.

Peer-median triangulation is not applicable to this analysis. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only four same-sector comparables overall, and applying narrow filters—same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier—yields fewer than three datapoints in any single cohort. A median derived from all-sector peers would introduce excessive heterogeneity and obscure meaningful valuation benchmarks specific to Aurora's market position and development stage.

Without sufficient homogenous comparable data, this valuation cannot be supported by standard triangulation methods at this time. Any defensible range would require either: (1) expansion of the peer set through identification of adjacent autonomous-systems or aerospace comparables with disclosed valuation multiples, or (2) application of alternative methods such as discounted cash flow or sum-of-the-parts analysis tied to Aurora's specific revenue projections and capital structure. The current dataset is insufficient to conclude a credible valuation range.
